seoTitle: Potato Point vs Pottery Estate — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Potato Point and Pottery Estate. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Potato Point vs Pottery Estate: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Potato Point and Pottery Estate.
heading: Potato Point vs Pottery Estate — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Potato Point, New South Wales and Pottery Estate, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Potato Point has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Pottery Estate (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Potato Point or Pottery Estate safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Potato Point has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Pottery Estate scores 45/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Potato Point scores 24/100 for bushfire risk. Pottery Estate scores 20/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Potato Point or Pottery Estate?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Potato Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
